activity_title: Phase 1 database and existing-asset discovery for WLSP
activity_summary: Ran read-only discovery across 14 user schemas. Catalogued core tables: order_transaction (1.22M rows), ppc_performance (24.5M), order_shipping_billing_detail (1.09M), amazon_returns (3772), amz_order_expenses (412K). Confirmed ppc_performance.ref_id=ASIN and record_type='ad' is the SKU grain. Concluded database GREEN/PASS to support all 5 leakage analyses (L1-L5).
validation_rule: All 5 analyses must be queryable from existing tables/views before any design

activity_title: Phase 2 review of existing development leakage framework
activity_summary: Inspected development.leakage_detection (404 rows; 404 OPEN / 0 CLOSED), leakage_classification (343), leakage_pattern_registry (7). Found pattern_id never populated (all NULL); root causes PRICE_TOO_LOW 178, SHIPPING_HIGH 148, FEE_ANOMALY 15, PAID_MEDIA_BLEED 2, RETURNS_ISSUE 0; daily_loss_gbp formula inconsistency between the 06-15 and 06-16 runs. Coverage vs protocol: L2/L3 PARTIAL, L1/L4/L5 MISSING.
validation_rule: Coverage verdict per analysis must cite a recorded query result
gap_or_risk: Existing engine has 0 closures (no feedback loop); pattern_id never wired to detections

activity_title: Duplicate-truth assessment: weekly protocol vs existing engine
activity_summary: Rated overlap between the weekly protocol and the existing engine: L3 net-negative vs leakage_detection (MEDIUM), L2 vs SHIPPING_HIGH classification (MEDIUM), L1 vs PAID_MEDIA_BLEED (LOW), L4 vs amazon_returns view (LOW). Core hazard identified: two systems issuing conflicting status for the same ASIN.
evidence_refs: evidence/query_results/PHASE2_RESULTS.md (14_detected_skus_vs_ppc: 2 of 94 open SKUs have active 7d PPC)
validation_rule: Every overlap rated with a de-duplication recommendation
gap_or_risk: Risk of two competing status sources per ASIN on L2/L3

activity_title: Built Mini-AIOS WLSP investigation workspace and recorded OPTION B architecture decision
activity_summary: Authored the full Mini-AIOS documentation set (README, START_HERE, REQ-001, options A/B/C, final decision, coverage matrix, queryability check, validation result, closure). Recorded architecture decision OPTION B: build a weekly report layer on top of the existing framework (reuse profitability views and pattern taxonomy, add L1/L4/L5 and the closure loop).
reusable_pattern: Mini-AIOS investigation workspace template: requirements/discovery/evidence/architecture_review/validation/closure with a document-control block per file
validation_rule: A new developer can trace every conclusion back to evidence (SQL + result + source object)
next_action: Bietrick to sign off OPTION B before any build

activity_title: Documentation validation audit of WLSP workspace
activity_summary: Audited all markdown for evidence backing. Verdict PASS WITH WARNINGS: no fabricated facts found; warnings on estimate-vs-exact labelling of row counts, unlabelled interpretive statements, one protocol-vs-observed PPC date discrepancy, and figure-transcription duplication across files. Listed 7 required corrections C-1 to C-7.
validation_rule: No fabricated facts; all measured claims traceable to a cited result
gap_or_risk: Interpretive statements not always labelled as inference; row counts are pg_stat estimates
next_action: Apply corrections C-1 to C-7 before the build phase

activity_summary: Field-by-field readiness audit: 36 required fields, 23 fully available, 13 partial, 0 missing (DB-required); overall readiness approx 82 percent. Corrected two prior figures with fresh queries: shipping coverage is 99.8 percent on completed orders (not 73.5), template price 21.2 percent. PASS - no analysis blocked by missing data.
validation_rule: PASS if all 5 supported with no absent DB-required field
gap_or_risk: PPC PH-mapping 40.6 percent missing (HIGH); refund-source conflict (HIGH); template 21.2 percent; order PH mapping 83.4 percent
next_action: Address DQ-1 and DQ-2 in the build; disclose partial-coverage caveats in the weekly report

activity_title: Root-cause analysis of HIGH findings DQ-1 (PPC PH gap) and DQ-2 (refund conflict)
activity_summary: DQ-1: PH-missing concentrates in 4 SKU-less accounts; raw ppc_etl_performance_data has no user_name column; only 11.3 percent of missing-PH SKUs recoverable via order_transaction; L1 impact is 22.8 percent of flagged spend (124.61 of 545.38 GBP) unattributed. DQ-2: order_transaction Refunded (50) vs amazon_returns FBM (303) measure different events - 242 returns still marked Completed; refunded_amount only 42.1 percent populated.
validation_rule: Each root cause proven by a cited query, not inferred
gap_or_risk: L1 PH grouping degraded; L4 must reconcile two refund sources
next_action: Use order_transaction as the L4 spine plus amazon_returns enrichment; run L1 at ASIN level

activity_summary: Searched the database and filesystem for the code that writes ppc_performance.user_name. No trigger, no SQL/PLpgSQL function references the PPC tables (18 plpgsql functions inspected; the 41 C functions are all dblink), and no ETL script exists on disk. Populator is external to the database and the workspace; it could not be shown from real code. Corrected the earlier inference that PH is a SKU-keyed lookup as UNPROVEN.
validation_rule: Show populator from real code OR prove its absence with evidence
gap_or_risk: user_name populator not accessible; lineage undocumented even in the column comment
next_action: Obtain the external PPC ETL repo/service and re-run the trace against its real code

activity_title: Memory-ingestion discovery: daily_task activity-memory standard and naming convention
activity_summary: Found the activity-memory standard in the daily_task schema: one table per developer/project, naming convention tbl_<projectcode>_<developer>. The 24-field standard is present on conforming tables and activity_id is the PRIMARY KEY on the conforming variant. Confirmed no tbl_wlsp_sarujanan existed and that tbl_ospm_sharujanan is a non-conforming older task-list schema (used as reference only).
validation_rule: Do not invent schema or naming; reuse the existing standard
gap_or_risk: Multiple inconsistent activity-table variants exist across daily_task

activity_title: Created and validated daily_task.tbl_wlsp_sarujanan activity-memory table
activity_summary: Created the approved per-project activity-memory table (26 columns; activity_id text PRIMARY KEY; imported_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()). Validated: table exists, PK = activity_id, 26 = 26 column parity with the standard (0 mismatches), 0 duplicate keys, 0 rows. Status GREEN and ready for MCP imports.
evidence_refs: CREATE TABLE daily_task.tbl_wlsp_sarujanan; validation queries V1-V5
reusable_pattern: Per-project activity-memory table cloned from the daily_task standard with activity_id PK as the idempotent UPSERT key
validation_rule: activity_id is PRIMARY KEY (NOT NULL + UNIQUE); column set identical to the standard
next_action: Load the 2026_06_22 work-log CSV via ON CONFLICT (activity_id) UPSERT after approval
